world.time: whole calendar days; listing-live = day 0; the committed kill window = calendar days 1..14 (T = 14 FIXED — the dial is V061's served territory); the committed 30-day signal window enters only as the cap constant of anchor A-CAP30; funnel-day d (d = 1, 2, ...) is calendar day tau + d
world.threshold: >= 1 organic sale inside the evidence window (the committed value; the qualified-inbound leg out of scope, P050's own scoping inherited)
world.tau_note: funnel onset in whole days after listing-live; 0 = the lived launch's measured same-day wiring (listing verified live 2026-07-12T16:25:16Z, funnel-top article live 2026-07-12T17:18:47Z — both timestamps committed in the same log); 20 = the committed base case (funnel misses the listing-anchored window entirely); tau is exogenous owner click latency, never optimized

SPIKE.adjective_verbatim: Show HN ... Spiky: 0 to a few hundred visits ... One shot

BURN.note: the DECISION shape — the lane's ONE actually-wired channel IS the slow-burn dev.to article

DRIP.adjective_verbatim: In-repo README buy badge ... Near-zero traffic, but free

world.shapes_note: pinned integer visit schedules, funnel-relative; magnitudes are invented-but-pinned readings of the channel table's own committed adjectives (docs/launch/distribution-channels.md @ 520bdfc), disclosed in the proposal and bracketed by the q and shape grids
world.q_note: per-visit i.i.d. conversion; decision cell 1/100 (~1% landing-page conversion, conservative, invented-but-pinned, grid-bracketed); dead product q = 0 (degeneracy row F5 — the rule's SPECIFICITY is perfect by construction; this head prices SENSITIVITY only)

A-LIST.predicate: committed — evidence window = calendar days 1..14 -> visible funnel-days d with tau + d <= 14

A-CAP30.predicate: funnel-anchored, hard-capped at the committed 30-day signal window — funnel-days d with d <= 14 AND tau + d <= 30

metrics.N: evidence mass N(shape, anchor, tau) = sum of scheduled visits inside the evidence window, exact integer
metrics.FK: false-kill probability FK = (1 - q)^N (zero sales among N i.i.d. Bernoulli(q) visits — exact rational bignum)
metrics.FK_profile: the FK(tau) profile per anchor at every shape and q
metrics.doubling_onset: min{tau on grid : FK(tau) >= 2*FK(0)} (exact Fraction comparison; 'off-grid-right' if no grid tau qualifies)
metrics.occupancy_row: slot occupancy per anchor per tau, per the anchor formulas above
metrics.queue_row: REPORTING-ONLY multiplication: expected false kills among the 10 publish-READY queued products at the decision cell, 10*FK(tau) per anchor (single-product arithmetic, no cross-product interaction — deliberately NOT the dropped release-cadence head)

REJECT.R1: FK(tau=13) >= 1/2 AND FK(tau=0) <= 1/5 (the SAME viable product flips risk class on click timing alone)
REJECT.R2: doubling onset min{tau in grid : FK(tau) >= 2*FK(0)} <= 13 (the doubling sits inside the window itself)
REJECT.R3: A-CAP30 holds FK(tau) = FK(0) EXACTLY at every grid tau <= 16 with worst-case slot occupancy <= 30 days (the theorem-gated conjunct, disclosed as such — falsifiability rides R1/R2)
REJECT.checked_first_because: the costly direction is fleet-wide: every calendar-anchored evidence deadline whose evidence channel is gated on a separate un-synchronized click inherits FK(tau)

decision_rule.INVALID: any F1-F6 gate failure (controls misbehaving — report, no ruling; short-circuits the token per the registered convention C7)

APPROVE.note: mutually exclusive with R1 by arithmetic (1/4 < 1/2, and FK(0) <= 1/5 makes (6/5)*FK(0) <= 6/25 < 1/2)

NULL_axes: band-straddle (FK(13) in (1/4, 1/2), or the doubling onset lands off-grid-right — the finding is the exact FK table)
shape-conditional (the promoted clause flips at SPIKE — drafting says SPIKE lands the APPROVE clauses exactly: the truncation tax is a slow-burn phenomenon, named, never ruling)
q-conditional (both q edges land outside R1, disclosed)
a theorem failure without gate failure (the drafter's window algebra is wrong — the corrected law is the finding)
twin-arm disagreement surviving the INVALID diagnosis

gates.F1: model identities — FK = (1-q)^N with N recomputed by both arms; conservation per cell (in-window + pre-window + post-window visits = shape total mass, exact); monotonicity (FK nonincreasing in q and in N); anchor coincidence at tau = 0 (all three anchors agree exactly, every cell)
gates.F2: the three structure theorems + monotone truncation, checked on the exact arithmetic across the FULL grid — (1) ANCHOR INVARIANCE: under A-FUNNEL the evidence window is funnel-days 1..14 for EVERY tau, so N and FK are exactly tau-invariant at every shape and every q; (2) SPIKE STEP: under A-LIST the spike's entire mass sits on funnel-day 1, so N = 200*1[tau <= 13] — FK constant at (1-q)^200 through tau = 13, exactly 1 at tau >= 14; (3) CAP-30 EQUIVALENCE + BOUNDEDNESS: A-CAP30 == A-FUNNEL exactly for all tau <= 16 (tau + 14 <= 30 <=> tau <= 16) with occupancy bounded by 30 (vs A-FUNNEL's unbounded tau + 14); truncation resumes only at tau > 16 (at tau = 20 the cap admits funnel-days 1..10: N = 174 at BURN); plus MONOTONE TRUNCATION: under A-LIST N(tau) nonincreasing in tau, zero at tau >= 14; FK nondecreasing, exactly 1 at tau >= 14 for every shape and q
gates.F3: closed-form anchors — the BURN pencil row N(tau) = (192, 179, 155, 125, 60, 0) across the tau grid under A-LIST from the cumulative schedule (60, 90, 110, 125, 137, 147, 155, 162, 168, 174, 179, 184, 188, 192); BURN total mass 261; DRIP N = 2*(14 - tau)^+ under A-LIST; (99/100)^5 = 9509900499/10000000000 exact; the CAP30 tau = 20 BURN cell N = 174
gates.F4: hand world — shape (3, 2, 1), window 3 days, q = 1/2, tau = 1: A-LIST admits funnel-days 1..2 -> N = 5, FK = 1/32 by pencil; the funnel anchor admits days 1..3 -> N = 6, FK = 1/64
gates.F5: degeneracies — q = 0 -> FK = 1 everywhere (the dead-product row: perfect specificity, disclosed boundary); q = 1 -> FK = 1[N = 0] (the anchor certainty edge); the tau = 20 A-LIST row = 1 exactly at every shape and q (the committed base case as arithmetic)
gates.F6: battery — Arm B exact-equal on every number; twin independently-written decision evaluators agree on the token; Arm-R draw-count sentinels counted and asserted; aux seed 20261583 never read (constructor registry); stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs; CPython minor pinned (3.11) and asserted

anchors.receipt: every anchor value above is copied verbatim from the PROPOSAL 066 F3/F4 lists; the runner re-derives each from the pinned formulas and asserts equality

arms.A: DECISION arm, seedless — exact-Fraction schedule-sum arithmetic over the full grid (3 shapes x 3 anchors x 6 tau x 3 q + degeneracy rows), byte-identical across process runs, stdlib only
arms.B: twin, seedless, INDEPENDENTLY-WRITTEN — a literal calendar day-walk simulator: walks calendar days 1..(tau + 365), deposits each day's visits per (shape, tau), tests window membership per anchor as a date comparison, accumulates N (and pre-/post-window mass for conservation), computes FK by literal repeated multiplication — must reproduce every Arm-A number EXACTLY; powers the second decision evaluator
arms.R: seeded, REPORTING-ONLY — the literal Bernoulli visit process at the decision cell (BURN, q = 1/100), anchors {A-LIST, A-CAP30} x tau in {0, 13}: 100,000 episodes on random.Random(20261580) (draw order: one uniform per in-window visit in schedule order, counts counted and asserted), stability leg random.Random(20261581) at 20,000, presentation shuffle 20261582 (read by the presentation-shuffled stdout grid listing only), aux 20261583 NEVER read; NO statistical gate rides Arm R — a trace drift from the exact FK values is a named finding, never a ruling

arm_R_params.draws_per_episode: exactly N(BURN, anchor, tau) uniforms — one per in-window visit in schedule order, counted and asserted per cell

harvested_sentences.anchor_sentence: T+14 (deadline) = 2026-07-26 — signal = >=1 organic sale OR >=1 qualified inbound within 14 days of the listing going live. No signal by T+14 -> ledger flag-E a NEGATIVE and pause / delist the product.
harvested_sentences.base_case_sentence: BASE CASE is 0 sales until a distribution channel is wired (the free gotcha article -> gist -> StackOverflow answer -> listing -> r/stripe funnel is still owner-gated), so payback is INDEFINITE absent traffic.

fixture_level_conventions.C1: Arm-R Bernoulli draw: u = rng.random(); a visit converts iff Fraction(u) < q strict (exact-rational comparison, no float thresholds); exactly one uniform per in-window visit in schedule order (pre-/post-window visits draw nothing — the evidence mass IS the priced object; N is deterministic per cell, so the per-cell sentinel is episodes * N exactly)
fixture_level_conventions.C2: Arm-R episode semantics: an episode is one product's evidence window at the cell; FK-hat = zero-sale episodes / episodes, reported beside the exact FK; per-episode draw count == N asserted
fixture_level_conventions.C3: Arm-B day-walk horizon: calendar days 1..(tau + 365) — a superset of the registered 1..60 walk with identical membership tests, extended so the twin also verifies conservation against DRIP's full 730-visit mass (the registered 60-day walk covers every window, whose latest possible end is calendar day 34, but not DRIP's total mass); N/FK identical either way, disclosed
fixture_level_conventions.C4: published exact surface scope: the full N table and FK table (3 shapes x 3 anchors x 6 tau x 3 q, Fractions as num/den strings + float renderings), the doubling-onset row per (shape, q) under A-LIST, the occupancy row per anchor per tau, degeneracy rows q in {0, 1}, theorem checks, hand world, the queue reporting row
fixture_level_conventions.C5: canonical serialization: exact rationals as reduced num/den decimal strings, floats via repr(), 6-decimal renderings via exact scaled-integer rounding (round-half-up on the exact Fraction, no float pathway), json sort_keys = True, no timestamps in results.json or stdout
fixture_level_conventions.C6: doubling-onset comparison is exact: FK(tau) >= 2*FK(0) as Fractions; a profile with no qualifying grid tau publishes the string 'off-grid-right'
fixture_level_conventions.C7: twin decision evaluators: evaluator A reads Arm-A numbers, evaluator B reads Arm-B numbers, independently-coded band logic, registered order REJECT -> INVALID -> APPROVE -> NULL; INVALID short-circuits on any gate failure (the V078 C7 convention)
fixture_level_conventions.C8: the aux-seed assertion is enforced by a constructor registry: every random.Random construction logs its seed; the registry must equal [20261580, 20261581, 20261582] exactly and never contain 20261583
fixture_level_conventions.C9: the drafter's disclosed 6-decimal floats are compared by rounding the exact Fraction to 6 decimal places (round-half-up); ratio disclosures (1.094x, 1.377x, 3.768x, 1.9608x, 87.8x) are compared at their stated precision; every mismatch is a first-class named anomaly in REPORT.md, never smoothed
fixture_level_conventions.C10: the R3/theorem-3 'every grid tau <= 16' set is {0, 3, 7, 10, 13} (the registered grid's members <= 16); worst-case occupancy is taken over the FULL tau grid including 20 (min(20+14, 30) = 30 <= 30)
fixture_level_conventions.C11: anomaly convention: drafter-disclosed constants re-derived and compared line-by-line (the V077/V078 A1/A2 precedent); no expression slip was pre-identified in P066's anchor text at registration — the drafter machine-checked the disclosed landing per the V078 session-card recommendation
